G1_sharpe_full_backtest_mean.note: V11-B3 (SMA-33962) still in_progress; no results/v11/metrics.json exists. Cannot evaluate without evidence.

G2_annualized_floor.note: No backtest or walk-forward output exists.

G5_framework_cv_oos_wf_sharpe.note: V11-B6 (walk-forward + framework CV) has not been started. The list of in_progress issues contains V11-B3 (SMA-33962) and V11-B7 (this review SMA-33964) but NO V11-B6 issue. Per the B7 spec, the predecessor B6 must show all gates passed; that condition is not satisfied.

G6_bootstrap_95_ci_lower.note: No bootstrap_ci.json exists. No walk-forward run means no bootstrap input data.

three_axis_contribution.evidence: trend + regime + POC are all hard-AND gates (any single failure -> return None). This is not a blend; it is a 3-axis gate. Per the issue brief: 'extra scrutiny on whether regime blend degenerates to single-axis' — without attribution analysis, we cannot confirm that the regime axis actually contributes beyond a simple trend+vol_breakout+POC gate. The regime_atr_pct gate fires BEFORE the vol_breakout check (line 555-557), so it could be doing real work, but per-axis attribution (ablation study) was not provided.

asymmetric_rr_1_2.evidence: vol_breakout_vpvr.py:508-509 — stop_loss_atr=1.0, take_profit_atr=2.0. The test (test_vol_breakout_vpvr.py:317) verifies 1.8 < RR < 2.2 for the produced Signal. Asymmetric RR 1:2 is correctly implemented at the strategy level.

multi_symbol_consistency.evidence: Strategy class does not have per-symbol logic — it runs identically on BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT. There are no per-symbol backtest results, so BTC/ETH/SOL divergence cannot be evaluated. The campaign spec says 'multi-symbol BTC/ETH/SOL all required' (V11-B3 spec), but B3 has not produced any per-symbol results.

critical.title: regime_atr_pct has no producer in the worktree
critical.description: VolBreakoutVPVRRegimeBlend4h.generate_signal reads data['regime_atr_pct'] (line 555), but a worktree-wide grep shows the symbol regime_atr_pct appears ONLY in the test file and the strategy file. No src/ module computes or injects this value. When V11-B3 runs against the real indicator pipeline, every bar will silently return None because data.get('regime_atr_pct') is None.
critical.impact: Backtest will produce zero trades on V11. Sharpe, profit_factor, max_dd all undefined. The strategy's regime axis is dead-on-arrival in production wiring.
critical.fix: Either (a) add a regime_atr_pct emitter to src/core/indicators/ (recommend extending vpvr_factor.py or a new regime factor module), OR (b) compute it from df directly inside the strategy: e.g. pct = df['atr'].rolling(N).rank(pct=True).iloc[-1] and threshold against regime_min_atr_pct. Option (b) keeps the strategy self-contained and is KISS-correct.

critical.title: higher_ema_50 column has no producer in the worktree
critical.description: All three V9/V10/V11 strategies read higher_tf_trend(df, ema_col='higher_ema_50') which calls df.iloc[-1].get(ema_col). The string 'higher_ema_50' is not produced by any module under src/ in the indicator-engineer worktree. The strategy relies on upstream pipeline code to inject this column, but the worktree shows no such producer. Like CR-1, the trend axis will silently fail and produce zero signals.
critical.impact: Same as CR-1: zero trades. The trend axis is dead-on-arrival in production wiring.
critical.fix: Compute the higher-TF EMA inside the strategy OR add an upstream producer. Recommend: pass a separate higher_tf_df via the data dict and compute ema_50 there. This makes the multi-TF contract explicit and testable.

major.title: '3-axis blend' is actually a 3-axis AND-gate, not a blend
major.description: The strategy docstring claims '3-axis blend' but the implementation is a sequential AND: trend != None AND regime_pct >= threshold AND vol_breakout fires AND POC proximity. A blend implies weighted attribution or confidence fusion (e.g. confidence = w_trend*trend_score + w_regime*regime_score + w_poc*poc_score). The current logic cannot show per-axis contribution because all axes are hard gates. To verify regime axis actually contributes, an ablation study (V11 with regime gate removed) is required — none provided.
major.impact: Cannot prove the regime axis is non-redundant. Could be that trend + vol_breakout + POC alone would give identical Sharpe.
major.fix: Either rename to '3-axis gate' (more accurate), OR implement a confidence-weighted blend: confidence = base * f_trend * f_regime * f_poc, where each f is in (0, 1]. Then ablate each axis to measure contribution.

major.title: regime_atr_pct read once — no persistence/stability check
major.description: regime_pct = data.get('regime_atr_pct') is a single-point threshold check. A 1h ATR percentile of 0.4 can be a momentary spike that reverses next bar. This causes regime flicker — strategy enters on a regime-confirmed bar and exits on a regime-denied bar. A persistence window (e.g. regime_pct >= threshold for last K bars) is standard practice and missing here.
major.impact: Regime flicker can drag Sharpe on noise. Especially risky on 1h regime for 15m entry.
major.fix: Add regime_persistence_bars param (default 3). Compute rolling min of last K regime_atr_pct values and require it >= threshold. If upstream is single-point, compute from df's atr history inside the strategy.

major.title: signal_id collision risk at millisecond resolution
major.description: f'vol_breakout_vpvr_regime_blend_4h_{int(datetime.utcnow().timestamp() * 1000)}' uses millisecond precision. On burst signals (multiple symbols in the same ms), ids collide. The downstream backtest engine typically dedupes by signal_id, which would silently drop one of the signals.
major.impact: Silent data loss on multi-symbol burst. Hard to debug.
major.fix: Append symbol + a monotonic counter: f'{strategy_name}_{symbol}_{int(ts*1000)}_{counter}'. Counter is per-strategy instance and incremented on every generate_signal call.

minor.title: datetime.utcnow() called twice in same Signal
minor.description: Two calls to datetime.utcnow() in close succession. Could disagree by 1ms. Minor but inconsistent with KISS — use a single ts variable.

minor.title: Default poc_k_atr=0.7 is tight; no sensitivity report
minor.description: POC proximity gate is |close - poc| / atr <= 0.7. With 15m noise this may be too tight. The strategy doesn't expose a sensitivity analysis (e.g. k_atr in {0.5, 0.7, 1.0, 1.5}). The campaign did not include a parameter sensitivity grid for V11.
minor.fix: Add a sensitivity report to B3 deliverables. Sweep k_atr in {0.5, 0.7, 1.0, 1.5} and report Sharpe/annualized/pf at each.

minor.title: Strategy has no explicit symbol-availability guard
minor.description: Signal uses data.get('symbol', 'UNKNOWN'). When symbol is missing, the Signal is emitted as 'UNKNOWN' — downstream backtest will not be able to attribute trades correctly. This should be a hard guard that returns None.
minor.fix: Add `if not data.get('symbol'): return None` early in generate_signal.

test_results.note: All unit tests pass. Tests cover skeleton helpers + each variant class init/required_indicators/registration/signal-logic gates. Tests do NOT cover wiring to upstream indicator pipeline (which is where CR-1 and CR-2 hit).

kiss_dry_solid_audit.KISS: PASS — three variants share ~80% skeleton (signal dataclass, vol-band math, ema/trend helper). Single file instead of three. Module docstring explicitly justifies this choice.
kiss_dry_solid_audit.DRY: PASS — compute_vol_bands, poc_proximity_ok, val_reject_fade, higher_tf_trend, _safe_float are shared helpers. No copy-paste across V9/V10/V11.
kiss_dry_solid_audit.SOLID: MIXED — Single Responsibility OK (one strategy per class). Open/Closed OK (BaseStrategy subclass). Liskov OK. Interface Segregation: validate_params + get_required_indicators + generate_signal is the minimum. Dependency Inversion: V11 depends on data dict keys (regime_atr_pct) and df columns (higher_ema_50) that are not produced by any known module — this violates the principle that high-level policy should not depend on undeclared low-level details.

security_audit.error_info_leakage: NONE — signal_id encodes only the variant name + timestamp; no PII / no exchange keys / no account info.

untested_code_paths: regime_atr_pct wiring (depends on upstream — see CR-1)
higher_ema_50 wiring (depends on upstream — see CR-2)
down direction: tests cover LONG only. SHORT path (lines 582-585) has the same logic but is not exercised.
real data path: tests use synthetic _force_breakout. No integration test with real OHLCV + VPVR + ATR.
edge cases: df with NaN, with inf, with single bar, with mismatched column dtypes.
unhandled_error_paths: data['df'] may have duplicate timestamps — compute_vol_bands will treat duplicates as separate bars; no de-dup.
data['df'] may have NaN in atr — _safe_float returns None, strategy returns None (graceful). OK.
data['df'] may have negative volume — vol_mean will be mean of negatives or partial; is_breakout still computes a threshold. Mathematically valid but semantically suspect.
data['regime_atr_pct'] may be a string (e.g. '0.5') — _safe_float coerces, OK.
datetime.utcnow() is deprecated in Python 3.12+ — recommend datetime.now(timezone.utc).

concrete_modification_suggestions.new:
# Self-contained regime: 1h ATR percentile from rolling window.
        # KISS-correct: avoid the undeclared contract on data['regime_atr_pct'].
        regime_window = self.params.get("regime_window_bars", 96)  # 1d at 15m
        if "atr" not in df.columns or len(df) < regime_window + 2:
            return None
        regime_pct = float(df["atr"].rolling(regime_window).rank(pct=True).iloc[-1])
        if not np.isfinite(regime_pct) or regime_pct < self.params["regime_min_atr_pct"]:
            return None
concrete_modification_suggestions.reason: CR-1 fix: make regime axis self-contained; remove the undeclared contract on data['regime_atr_pct'].

concrete_modification_suggestions.new:
# Self-contained higher-TF trend: 4h ema_50 = close.rolling(16).mean() at 15m.
        # KISS-correct: avoid undeclared contract on df['higher_ema_50'].
        higher_tf_period = self.params.get("higher_tf_ema_period", 16)  # 4h at 15m
        if len(df) < higher_tf_period + 2:
            return None
        ema_col = df["close"].ewm(span=higher_tf_period, adjust=False).mean().iloc[-1]
        close = _safe_float(df["close"].iloc[-1])
        if ema_col is None or close is None or not np.isfinite(ema_col):
            return None
        trend = "up" if close > ema_col else ("down" if close < ema_col else None)
        if trend is None:
            return None
concrete_modification_suggestions.reason: CR-2 fix: make trend axis self-contained; remove the undeclared contract on df['higher_ema_50'].
MR-3 fix: prevent collision by including symbol + monotonic counter; switch to datetime.now(timezone.utc) (utcnow is deprecated in 3.12+).
